steps.title: Graph the vertex model
steps.content: The maximum height is at $t=3$, so the axis of symmetry is $t=3$. To make this visible, fit $h=a(t-3)^2+51.1$ using the launch point $(0,7)$ and the vertex $(3,51.1)$, then graph the horizontal line $y=7$.

steps.title: Compute the return time
steps.content: The graph intersects $y=7$ at $t=0$ and again at $t=6$. By symmetry, the two equal-height times must average to the vertex time: $\frac{0+t_2}{2}=3$, so $t_2=6$. That matches choice B.
